---
title: ""
author: "Giorgio Arcara"
date: ""
#format: revealjs
format:
  beamer:
    theme: metropolis
    keep-tex: false
incremental: false
header-includes:
  \setbeamerfont{title}{size=\large}
  \usepackage{hyperref}
  \setbeamertemplate{footline}[frame number]
---

<!-- the section below is shown only if rendering is beamer from latex. (and format:revealjs is commmented) -->

::: {.content-visible when-format="beamer"}
```{=latex}
% TITLE SLIDES
\title{{\fontsize{15pt}{16pt}\selectfont Applied Psychometrics}:\\ studying subclinical psychopathologies\\ \vspace{1.5em} \raggedright 
\emph{3.1 - Selecting Tests: Goals}}
\author{Giorgio Arcara,\\ University of Padua \\ IRCCS San Camillo, Venice}

\titlegraphic{

\vspace*{7cm}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/LogoSanCamilloIRCCS_Unipd_alpha.png}
\begin{center}
\vspace{-2.2em}
\includegraphics[scale=0.15]{Figures/CC_license_3_0.png}
\end{center}
}
\date{2026}
\maketitle
```
:::

## sometimes we forget the clinical goal
\centering

![](Figures/target.png){width="50%"}



## selecting tests

- Our toolkit should include tests we will need for our 

**potential clinical goals** \pause

- Our assessment should include tests that are useful to our

**specific clinical goals**

## Three intersecting aspects
\centering
![](Figures/Selecting_Tests.png){width="70%"}



## Goals and Psychometric Properties

Depending on the goal, we will select appropriate tests (e.g. _validity_, or thresholds for detecting change)

## Goals and Practical Aspects

Depending on practical aspects we may prefer one test or another (e.g. in some clinical settings one test can be preferred)
